records.name: Shared Engine Portfolio Game Contract
records.description: Shared game-core product contract used by Unity, Godot, and Unreal compatibility fixtures.

records.name: Engine Portfolio Baseline Game
records.description: Engine-neutral game product contract used to prove side-by-side Unity, Godot, and Unreal workspace compatibility.
records.pitch: Players test a compact prototype loop that can be implemented in Unity, Godot, or Unreal from the same product intent.
records.playerFantasy: A developer can compare engine implementation paths without losing the shared game-design contract.
records.targetAudience: Engine-tooling maintainers and game prototyping teams evaluating cross-engine implementation readiness.

records.name: Engine Portfolio Session
records.description: Single-player prototype mode for validating equivalent engine implementation records.
records.summary: A compact session where the same prototype behavior is described once and implemented by each engine workspace.

records.name: Validate, Compare, Ship
records.description: Engine-neutral loop for validating equivalent product-contract coverage across engine workspaces.

steps.name: Specify
steps.description: Capture the shared prototype intent in game-core records.

steps.name: Implement
steps.description: Map the shared intent to engine-specific project and tooling records.

steps.name: Validate
steps.description: Run validation, lint, readiness, and graph checks for each engine workspace.

steps.name: Compare
steps.description: Review the portfolio workspace graph for side-by-side engine coverage.

records.name: Engine Portfolio Prototype Slice
records.description: Prototype scope that proves shared game intent can be consumed by equivalent Unity, Godot, and Unreal workspaces.
records.goal: Demonstrate that engine workspaces can consume one exported game-core contract through a local dependency alias.
successCriteria: Each engine workspace passes strict lint and readiness checks.
The portfolio workspace graph includes all three engine projects and the exported shared game-core records.
